Patch Set 9:

Is there any provision for sharing code between kernel and userspace, without resorting to large static inline functions in headers?  A bunch of stuff like struct accessors, nid2str/str2nid, etc. could be shared again if there was a clean way to do so.

Patch Set 9:

> Patch Set 9:
> 
> Is there any provision for sharing code between kernel and userspace, without resorting to large static inline functions in headers?  A bunch of stuff like struct accessors, nid2str/str2nid, etc. could be shared again if there was a clean way to do so.

Running something like:

git grep __KERNEL__ -- ':(exclude)*.h'

on latest Linus tree shows a handful of examples. For struct accessors, uapi seems to be the best place. I doubt those are super large?

For stuff like nid2str, I think canonical version would live in the kernel and the userspace library would copy the file over and compile it into libcfs. libcfs would still live in lib/, so we can still get rid of the weird automake stuff from the kernel build.

Do you have a list of what's duplicated?

Patch Set 9:

(99 comments)

109 style warning(s).
For more details please see https://wiki.lustre.org/Lustre_Coding_Style_Guidelines

Topic set to copy-builtin

Patch Set 11:

Andreas, about making the libcfs userspace consume the in-kernel NID string definitions:

After thinking about it, I think we should be aiming to decouple userspace and kernel more. Eventually, sites could use the latest up-to-date userspace tools on any version of Lustre. But consuming the in-kernel NID string definitions would add a build time dependency on the kernel where it is not needed.

Patch Set 11: Code-Review+1

Personally I like to see libcfs_netstrfns go away. Instead LND drivers should properly register with LNet and use exported functions like libcfs_ip_addr2str() instead. This
avoid placing LND specifics inside of LNet core code. Currently I could see people examine libcfs_netstrfns and see the bull driver define and ask where is the driver? We could avoid that issue if we had a registeration system.

Patch Set 11:

James, we don't need the driver in order to route to/from a remote network on that network, only the LND name-to-number mapping.

parents.subject: LU-18818 utils: covert to libext2fs for rest of ldiskfs ops

commit.subject: LU-18687 lib: move libcfs userspace library
commit.message:
LU-18687 lib: move libcfs userspace library

Move the libcfs userspace library to the top-level
lib directory. Eventually, all of the userspace
tools and libraries will live alongside it.

We move *.c files:
from: libcfs/libcfs/util/*.c
to: lib/libcfs/*.c

We move *.h files:
from: libcfs/include/libcfs/util/*.h
to: include/libcfs/util/*.h

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Timothy Day <timday@amazon.com>
Change-Id: Ic53a56097cb55d7247b5f1956d0f4bc45bf34680
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58682
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
